solution.p: In this exercise, 4x is the greatest common factor. We find

Note that you might fail to recognize that 4x is the greatest common factor. At first you might only see that, say, 4 is a common factor. This is OK! You can factor out the 4 and then keep looking for more to factor out:
Now examine the polynomial that remained in the parentheses. There is still a common factor, x\text{.} Factoring this out too:
So there is more than one way to find the answer.
